components.name: ActionRow
components.summary: The one canonical action/command row (icon, label, description, chevron); gating-aware.

props.name: icon
props.description: Leading glyph, e.g. Icon.search({ size: 18 }).

props.name: label
props.description: The action name; the primary line and accessible name.

props.name: description
props.description: Secondary line beneath the label.

props.name: tone
props.description: Semantic colour accent for the row.

props.name: selected
props.description: Highlights the row as the current selection (keyboard / active item).

props.name: reason
props.description: When disabled, the gating reason shown to explain why.

props.name: onClick
props.description: Invoked when the row is activated.

components.usage: <ActionRow icon={Icon.search({ size: 18 })} label="Search records" description="Find a patient" onClick={run} />

components.name: AIBadge
components.summary: Companion brain mark (inline SVG, token gradient); optional glow.

props.name: size
props.description: Pixel size of the mark.

props.name: glow
props.description: Adds the token-driven glow treatment for emphasis.

components.name: AIMarker
components.summary: AI-generated / verify provenance marker.

props.name: text
props.description: The disclosure text, e.g. "AI-generated - verify before acting".

components.name: AlertDialog
components.summary: Modal that forces a consequential confirmation (Base UI alert-dialog).

props.name: trigger
props.description: Element that opens the alert (a Btn). Omit when controlling open/onOpenChange yourself.

props.name: title
props.description: The decision, phrased as a question ("Delete prescription?"); the accessible name.

props.name: description
props.description: The consequence, stated plainly ("This cannot be undone."); wired as aria-describedby.

props.name: children
props.description: Optional extra body content beyond the description.

props.name: actions
props.description: The cancel + confirm controls; wrap cancel in AlertDialogClose and make it the safe default.

props.name: className
props.description: Extra class(es) appended to the surface.

components.usage:
<AlertDialog title="Delete prescription?" description="This cannot be undone."
  trigger={<Btn variant="secondary">Delete</Btn>}
  actions={<><AlertDialogClose><Btn variant="secondary">Cancel</Btn></AlertDialogClose><Btn tone="error">Delete</Btn></>} />

bestPractices.text: Name the consequence in the description; give the confirm Btn tone="error" and label it with the verb ("Delete"), not "OK".
Use an AlertDialog for routine confirmations - reserve it for irreversible or costly actions, or it becomes noise.

anatomy.name: Backdrop
anatomy.description: The scrim; blocks interaction until a choice is made.

anatomy.name: Title
anatomy.description: The decision question, the accessible name.

components.name: AlertDialogClose
components.summary: The cancel / dismiss control inside an AlertDialog; wrap a Btn.

props.name: children
props.description: The control that dismisses the alert (a Btn); make it the safe default.

components.name: AllergyCard
components.summary: FHIR AllergyIntolerance card. Criticality maps to status interpretation (critical/borderline).

props.name: data
props.description: The FHIR AllergyIntolerance: { substance, criticality, reactions, recordedDate, lastVerified }. substance is the title; criticality drives the status pill.

props.name: stage
props.description: Fidelity forwarded to the underlying Card. Sprout and Shoot render the reaction list; Sprout adds recorded/verified dates; Shoot exposes the severity-update CTA.

props.name: onEdit
props.description: Invoked when the user enters edit, from the Shoot edit chip or the "Ernst bijwerken" CTA.

components.name: Box
components.summary: Primitive layout container: token-driven padding / surface / radius / border.

props.name: p
props.description: Padding step, resolves to var(--space-N). px/py set an axis; pt/pr/pb/pl set one side.

props.name: px
props.description: Horizontal padding step (left + right).

props.name: py
props.description: Vertical padding step (top + bottom).

props.name: bg
props.description: Surface token name; resolves to var(--<bg>), e.g. bg="surface-raised".

props.name: radius
props.description: Radius token step; resolves to var(--krnl-radius-<radius>), e.g. radius="sm".

props.name: border
props.description: Adds a hairline token border (--border-subtle).

props.name: as
props.description: The HTML element to render.

props.name: children
props.description: The laid-out content.

bestPractices.text: Use Box for a padded / surfaced region; reach for Stack / Inline / Grid when it also arranges children.
Hardcode padding in px - pass a space step (p={3}) so spacing stays on the tokenset.

components.name: Btn
components.summary: Deprecated alias of Button (B-39). Use Button; Btn is removed on the next major.

components.name: Button
components.summary: Text button with variant (emphasis) + tone (semantic color).

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual emphasis. Reserve primary for the single most important action in a view. `bare` strips all button paint (weight, padding, border, fill, hover) so a bespoke/domain class layered via className fully owns the look - the escape hatch for one-off styled controls that still need real button behaviour + a11y + ref-forwarding.

props.name: tone
props.description: Semantic color, orthogonal to variant. neutral = the default action accent; error = a destructive confirm (e.g. Delete). Maps to --status-{tone}-solid/on/tint; neutral has no status token and keeps the default button colors.

props.name: size
props.description: Size step; resolves to the density / size-step tokens.

props.name: children
props.description: The button label. Write the action ("Save changes"), not "OK" or "Click here".

bestPractices.text: Reserve primary for the single most important action in the view; use secondary for the rest.
Write labels that describe the action ("Save changes", "Delete account"), not "OK".
Place more than one primary button in the same view - it dilutes the hierarchy.
Use a Button for navigation; if it only goes to another page, use a link.

anatomy.name: Label
anatomy.description: The visible text (children); also the accessible name.

anatomy.name: Icon
anatomy.description: An optional leading icon reinforcing the label.

examples.name: Secondary
examples.description: Lower emphasis; pairs with a primary.

examples.name: Destructive
examples.description: error tone for an irreversible action; pair it with an AlertDialog confirm.

components.name: Card
components.summary: Slot-driven base card for the Sprout pattern. Three fidelities (seed/sprout/shoot), two colour axes (tone/status), container-query intrinsic width morph.

props.name: stage
props.description: Fidelity: seed (glance), sprout (detail), shoot (edit). Drives the intrinsic width morph.

props.name: tone
props.description: Identity colour axis; drives the --card-tone-* bindings. Independent of status.

props.name: status
props.description: Clinical interpretation; colours the StatusPill, orthogonal to the identity tone.

props.name: leading
props.description: Leading slot (an IconPill or avatar).

props.name: title
props.description: Card heading slot.

props.name: value
props.description: Primary value slot (typically a ValueDisplay).

props.name: actions
props.description: Trailing action slot (Btn / EditChip); RTL-safe.

props.name: onClick
props.description: Invoked when the card is activated.

components.usage: <Card stage="sprout" tone="heart" title="Heart rate" value={<ValueDisplay value="72" unit="bpm" />} />

components.name: Checkbox
components.summary: Single boolean checkbox with an optional label (Base UI checkbox).

props.name: label
props.description: Text beside the box; the whole row toggles. Omit for a bare box (label it elsewhere).

bestPractices.text: Use for independent on/off options; use a RadioGroup when exactly one of several must be chosen.
Write the label as a positive statement ("Email me updates"), so checked = the stated thing is true.
Use a Checkbox to switch a setting on immediately - that is a Toggle (switch).

anatomy.name: Box
anatomy.description: The square control carrying the checked/indeterminate state.

components.name: Chip
components.summary: Small token-styled label - suggestion, filter, removable tag, or citation.

props.name: children
props.description: The chip label (keep it short).

props.name: icon
props.description: Optional leading glyph; decorative (aria-hidden).

props.name: selected
props.description: Marks the chosen state for a filter / choice chip (sets aria-pressed on the actionable form).

props.name: onClick
props.description: Makes the chip actionable (renders a button). Use for suggestion / filter / citation chips.

props.name: onRemove
props.description: Adds a trailing remove control (renders a static label + a remove button). Use for a removable tag; do not combine with onClick.

bestPractices.text: Keep the label to a word or two; a chip is a token, not a sentence.
Pick one mode: actionable (onClick) or removable (onRemove) - not both (avoids a button inside a button).
Use a Chip as a primary action - that is a Button. Chips are lightweight, secondary, and plural.

anatomy.name: Remove
anatomy.description: A trailing remove button when onRemove is set.

examples.name: Removable tag
examples.description: Static label with a remove control.

examples.name: Filter (selected)
examples.description: Actionable filter chip in the chosen state.

components.name: Collapsible
components.summary: A single region that expands and collapses (Base UI collapsible).

props.name: trigger
props.description: The always-visible header that toggles the region (a chevron is added automatically).

props.name: children
props.description: The content revealed when open.

bestPractices.text: Use to hide secondary detail behind a clear header; keep the header label descriptive of what expands.
Use several stacked Collapsibles where only one should be open at a time - use an Accordion for that.

components.name: ConditionCard
components.summary: FHIR Condition card. Status pill follows clinicalStatus mapped to interpretation.

props.name: data
props.description: The FHIR Condition: { code, clinicalStatus, severity, onsetDate, verification, note }. code is the title; clinicalStatus drives the status pill; the rest render as a field list.

props.name: stage
props.description: Fidelity forwarded to the underlying Card. Sprout and Shoot render the onset/severity/verification field list; Sprout adds the note; Shoot exposes the status-update CTA.

props.name: onEdit
props.description: Invoked when the user taps the edit chip in Shoot stage.

props.name: onStatusChange
props.description: Invoked with the condition when the user triggers the Shoot-stage status-update CTA.

components.name: ContextMenu
components.summary: Right-click / long-press contextual menu on a region (Base UI context-menu).

props.name: trigger
props.description: The region whose context-menu gesture (right-click / long-press) opens the menu, e.g. a card or list row.

props.name: children
props.description: The menu body: ContextMenuItem rows, divided by ContextMenuSeparator.

props.name: className
props.description: Extra class(es) appended to the popup surface.

bestPractices.text: Mirror the primary actions available elsewhere; a context menu is a shortcut, not the only path to an action.
Hide critical actions behind a context menu only - discoverability on touch is poor.

anatomy.name: Trigger region
anatomy.description: The area that responds to the context-menu gesture.

anatomy.name: Popup
anatomy.description: The portalled surface holding the items (shared .krnl-menu-* look).

components.name: ContextMenuItem
components.summary: A single command row inside a ContextMenu.

props.name: icon
props.description: Optional leading icon; decorative (aria-hidden).

props.name: children
props.description: The command label (the action).

props.name: onClick
props.description: Fires on activation; the menu then closes.

components.name: ContextMenuSeparator
components.summary: A hairline divider between ContextMenu item clusters.

components.name: DemographicCard
components.summary: FHIR Patient identity card. Seed/Sprout/Shoot. No time-series; attributes flow as a field list.

props.name: data
props.description: The FHIR Patient: { name, birthDate, age, gender, identifier, gp, phone, address, lastVisit }. name is the title; the remaining attributes render as a field list.

props.name: stage
props.description: Fidelity forwarded to the underlying Card. Seed shows age only; Sprout and Shoot render the attribute field list; Sprout adds the last-contact footer; Shoot exposes the save CTA.

props.name: onEdit
props.description: Invoked when the user taps the edit chip in Shoot stage.

props.name: onSave
props.description: Invoked with the patient when the user triggers the Shoot-stage save CTA.

components.name: Dialog
components.summary: Modal surface for a focused task or confirmation (Base UI dialog).

props.name: trigger
props.description: Optional element that opens the dialog (a Btn). Omit when controlling open/onOpenChange yourself.

props.name: title
props.description: The dialog heading; wired as the accessible name (aria-labelledby).

props.name: description
props.description: Supporting line under the title; wired as aria-describedby.

props.name: children
props.description: The dialog body content.

props.name: actions
props.description: Footer controls, typically a DialogClose-wrapped cancel and a confirm Btn; right-aligned.

props.name: className
props.description: Extra class(es) appended to the dialog surface (e.g. a wider variant).

bestPractices.text: Give every dialog a title and a clear primary action; put the primary Btn last in actions.
Use a modal Dialog for a non-blocking message - use a Banner or Toast. Reserve modality for tasks that need full attention.

anatomy.name: Backdrop
anatomy.description: The scrim behind the dialog; dims and blocks the page.

examples.name: Confirm
examples.code:
<Dialog title="Discard changes?" description="Your edits will be lost."
  trigger={<Btn variant="secondary">Discard</Btn>}
  actions={<><DialogClose><Btn variant="secondary">Keep editing</Btn></DialogClose><Btn>Discard</Btn></>} />

components.name: DialogClose
components.summary: A close control inside a Dialog; wrap a Btn / IconButton.

props.name: children
props.description: The control that closes the dialog on click (a Btn / IconButton).

components.name: EditChip
components.summary: Pencil + label trailing chip used on Shoot-stage cards. Composes Base UI Button for the a11y and focus baseline.

props.name: label
props.description: The chip text shown beside the pencil glyph.

props.name: onClick
props.description: Invoked when the chip is activated (enters edit mode).

components.name: Fab
components.summary: Floating action button for one persistent, high-emphasis action.

props.name: children
props.description: The icon or mark shown in the button (e.g. an AIBadge or an Icon).

props.name: pulse
props.description: Adds the token-driven attention pulse ring (e.g. to promote a new capability).

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Accessible name; required since the Fab is icon-only.

bestPractices.text: Use exactly one Fab per surface for the single most important standing action; always give it an aria-label.
Stack multiple Fabs or use one for a routine action - it is the loudest control on the screen.

anatomy.name: Pulse
anatomy.description: An optional attention ring behind the mark.

components.name: FFSection
components.summary: Collapsible Feature-flags section (Base UI Accordion item).

props.name: title
props.description: Section heading shown on the accordion trigger.

props.name: children
props.description: The flag controls revealed when the section is open.

components.name: FieldList
components.summary: Definition list of label / value rows for demographic and condition detail bodies.

props.name: items
props.description: The rows to render. Each label becomes a dt and its value a dd; both accept nodes, not just strings.

components.name: FileInput
components.summary: Native file picker; usually hidden and triggered via a ref (forwardRef).

components.name: Grid
components.summary: CSS grid with a column count (or template) and a token gap.

props.name: columns
props.description: Column count (=> repeat(n, minmax(0,1fr))) or an explicit grid-template-columns string.

props.name: gap
props.description: Gap between cells; resolves to var(--space-N).

props.name: p
props.description: Padding step (var(--space-N)); px/py/pt.. also accepted.

props.name: children
props.description: The grid cells.

components.name: IconButton
components.summary: Square icon button; Base UI render-target (forwardRef).

props.name: active
props.description: Renders the pressed / active visual state (e.g. a toggled toolbar button).

props.name: children
props.description: The icon element, e.g. Icon.close({ size: 18 }).

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Accessible name; required since there is no visible label.

components.name: IconPill
components.summary: Rounded-square tone-tinted glyph holder for the card leading slot.

props.name: children
props.description: The glyph to render (an icon element); tinted by the card tone.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible name for the pill, which carries an image role.

components.name: Inline
components.summary: Horizontal flow with a token gap; items vertically centered by default (flex row).

props.name: gap
props.description: Space between children; resolves to var(--space-N).

props.name: align
props.description: Cross-axis alignment (align-items); defaults to center.

props.name: justify
props.description: Main-axis distribution (justify-content).

props.name: wrap
props.description: Allow items to wrap to the next line.

props.name: p
props.description: Padding step (var(--space-N)); px/py/pt.. also accepted.

props.name: children
props.description: The inline content.

components.name: LabResultCard
components.summary: FHIR Observation (laboratory) Seed/Sprout. Reference range central; Shoot rare (lab acknowledge pushed to Rooted).

props.name: data
props.description: The FHIR Observation projection: { property, value, unit, status, low, high, measuredAt, trend }. low/high define the reference range rendered by the ReferenceRangeBar in Sprout.

props.name: stage
props.description: Fidelity forwarded to the underlying Card. Sprout renders the reference-range bar plus range/measured footer. Shoot is rare for labs and typically deferred to Rooted.

props.name: onAcknowledge
props.description: Optional handler for acknowledging an out-of-range result; lab acknowledge is generally routed to Rooted.

components.name: MedicationCard
components.summary: FHIR MedicationStatement. Schedule strip in Sprout; mark-taken in Shoot. Prescribing requires Rooted.

props.name: data
props.description: The FHIR MedicationStatement: { name, dose, frequency, medicationStatus, schedule, reason, prescriber }.

props.name: stage
props.description: Fidelity (forwarded to the underlying Card).

props.name: onEdit
props.description: Invoked when the user enters edit (Shoot stage).

props.name: onMarkTaken
props.description: Invoked when the user marks a dose taken.

components.name: Menu
components.summary: Anchored, dismissible dropdown menu of commands (Base UI menu).

props.name: trigger
props.description: The element that opens the menu (a Btn / IconButton). Used as the Base UI render target, so it must be a forwardRef component or the popup anchors at (0,0).

props.name: children
props.description: The menu body: MenuItem rows, optionally grouped with MenuGroup / MenuGroupLabel and divided by MenuSeparator.

props.name: className
props.description: Extra class(es) appended to the popup surface, for one-off width or spacing tweaks (e.g. a wide variant).

bestPractices.text: Use for a set of actions on the same object; give the trigger an accessible name (aria-label) when it is icon-only.
Group related commands with MenuGroup + MenuGroupLabel; divide groups (and set off a destructive action) with a MenuSeparator - groups do not draw their own divider.
Use a Menu to pick one value from a set - that is a Select. A Menu fires actions, it does not hold a value.
Put more than a screen of items in a Menu; for long lists reach for a Select or a Combobox.

anatomy.name: Trigger
anatomy.description: The forwardRef element that opens the menu (Btn / IconButton).

anatomy.name: Popup
anatomy.description: The portalled, positioned surface holding the items.

anatomy.name: Item
anatomy.description: A single command row (MenuItem); fires onClick and closes the menu.

anatomy.name: Group + GroupLabel
anatomy.description: A labelled cluster of related items.

examples.name: Grouped
examples.description: Related commands under section labels; a MenuSeparator divides the groups.

components.name: MenuGroup
components.summary: A labelled cluster of related Menu items.

props.name: children
props.description: A MenuGroupLabel followed by the MenuItems in the cluster.

components.name: MenuGroupLabel
components.summary: The heading for a MenuGroup.

props.name: children
props.description: The section heading text; associated with its group for screen readers by Base UI.

components.name: MenuItem
components.summary: A single command row inside a Menu.

props.name: icon
props.description: Optional leading icon reinforcing the command; decorative (aria-hidden), the label is the accessible name.

props.name: children
props.description: The command label. Write the action ("Delete prescription"), not "OK".

props.name: onClick
props.description: Fires when the item is activated (click or keyboard); the menu closes automatically afterwards.

components.name: MenuSeparator
components.summary: A hairline divider between Menu item clusters.

components.name: MeterTooltip
components.summary: Tooltip wrapper that exposes measurement metadata on hover (timestamp, source). Composes Base UI Tooltip.

props.name: tip
props.description: The metadata content shown in the popup; when absent the children render bare with no tooltip.

props.name: children
props.description: The trigger element the tooltip is attached to (a value or status pill).

components.name: PAv
components.summary: Deprecated alias of PersonAvatar (B-39). Use PersonAvatar; PAv is removed on the next major.

components.name: PersonAvatar
components.summary: Person avatar with initials fallback.

props.name: p
props.description: The person record; the name drives the initials fallback when no photo is available.

props.name: size
props.description: Pixel diameter of the (always circular) avatar.

anatomy.name: Photo
anatomy.description: The person image, shown when available.

anatomy.name: Initials
anatomy.description: One or two letters from the name; shown when there is no photo.

components.name: Popover
components.summary: Anchored, dismissible surface for rich content (Base UI popover).

props.name: trigger
props.description: The element that opens the popover (a Btn / IconButton). Base UI render target - must be forwardRef or the popup anchors at (0,0).

props.name: title
props.description: Optional heading; wired as the accessible name (aria-labelledby) by Base UI.

props.name: children
props.description: The popover body - any content (text, fields, PopoverDescription, a PopoverClose).

props.name: className
props.description: Extra class(es) appended to the popup surface (e.g. a wider variant).

bestPractices.text: Use for rich, interactive content anchored to a control (a filter form, a detail card).
Use a Popover for a simple label - that is a Tip (tooltip). Use it for a command list - that is a Menu.

anatomy.name: Trigger
anatomy.description: The forwardRef element that opens the popover.

anatomy.name: Body
anatomy.description: The content (children); may include PopoverDescription and PopoverClose.

examples.name: With close
examples.code:
<Popover title="Profile" trigger={<IconButton aria-label="Profile">{Icon.user({ size: 18 })}</IconButton>}>
  <PopoverDescription>Signed in as Dr. Vermeulen.</PopoverDescription>
  <PopoverClose><Btn variant="secondary">Close</Btn></PopoverClose>
</Popover>

components.name: PopoverClose
components.summary: A close control inside a Popover; wrap a Btn / IconButton.

props.name: children
props.description: The control that closes the popover on click (a Btn / IconButton).

components.name: PopoverDescription
components.summary: A descriptive paragraph inside a Popover (wires aria-describedby).

props.name: children
props.description: The descriptive text.

components.name: PrimaryCTA
components.summary: Primary action button for the card actions slot; composes Base UI Button for the focus and keyboard baseline, styled via tokens.

props.name: children
props.description: The button label. Write the action it performs (e.g. "Save value"), not "OK".

props.name: onClick
props.description: Invoked when the button is activated by pointer or keyboard.

components.name: Progress
components.summary: Determinate progress of a task toward completion (Base UI progress).

props.name: label
props.description: Caption above the track; also the accessible name. If omitted you must supply aria-label instead.

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Accessible name when there is no visible label. Exactly one of label / aria-label is required - Base UI names the progressbar only from a mounted label, so a bare bar is otherwise unnamed (WCAG 4.1.2).

bestPractices.text: Give every Progress a name: a visible label, or aria-label when the bar stands alone. Without one the progressbar is unnamed.
Use for a task with a knowable percentage (upload, import); pair with a value so progress is legible.
Use Progress to report a static level or score (battery, risk) - that is a Meter. Progress implies movement toward done.

anatomy.name: Indicator
anatomy.description: The fill, sized to value/max by Base UI.

components.name: PropertyList
components.summary: Collection view for sprout-cards. Three layouts (rows / grid / columns); cards keep their intrinsic width-morph. Header slots for filter / sort / action.

props.name: title
props.description: Optional list heading; also the fallback accessible name when ariaLabel is absent. Shown with a live item count.

props.name: items
props.description: The data records to render. Each is passed to renderItem; item.id or item.key is used as the list key.

props.name: renderItem
props.description: Render prop (item, index) returning the card for one record. The list is agnostic about which Card component it returns.

props.name: layout
props.description: Arrangement: rows (single-column stack), grid (auto-fit cells at least minCard wide), columns (CSS multi-column flow). Layout belongs to the list, not the card.

props.name: minCard
props.description: Minimum cell width in px for grid, or the column-width for columns. Drives the --krnl-list-min-card var so the consumer never edits the stylesheet.

props.name: density
props.description: Overrides the page density token for this list only, via the data-density attribute.

props.name: filter
props.description: Header slot for a filter control the host composes.

props.name: sort
props.description: Header slot for a sort control the host composes.

props.name: action
props.description: Header slot for a primary action (e.g. an add button), placed at the end of the header.

props.name: emptyLabel
props.description: Text shown in place of the items when items is empty.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description: Accessible name for the list region; falls back to title when omitted.

props.name: className
props.description: Extra class names appended after the canonical list + layout classes.

anatomy.name: Header
anatomy.description: Shown when a title, filter, sort, or action is present.

anatomy.name: Title
anatomy.description: The list heading plus a live item count.

anatomy.name: Items
anatomy.description: The rendered cards, one per record via renderItem.

anatomy.name: Empty
anatomy.description: The emptyLabel message shown when there are no items.

components.name: Radio
components.summary: One option inside a RadioGroup.

props.name: label
props.description: The clickable text beside the dot.

components.name: RadioGroup
components.summary: Pick exactly one option from a small set (Base UI radio-group).

props.name: children
props.description: The Radio options.

bestPractices.text: Use for 2-5 mutually exclusive options that are all worth showing at once; above ~6, use a Select.
Use a RadioGroup for independent on/off options - those are Checkboxes.

components.name: ReactionList
components.summary: Bulleted list of allergy reactions, each with a leading dot marker.

props.name: reactions
props.description: The reaction descriptions to list, one bullet each (e.g. hives, anaphylaxis).

components.name: ReferenceRangeBar
components.summary: Horizontal reference-range strip: a normal band plus a marker for the measured value, colour-coded in or out of range.

props.name: value
props.description: The measured value; positions the marker and decides its in-range or out-of-range styling.

props.name: low
props.description: Lower bound of the normal band, shown as the left scale tick.

props.name: high
props.description: Upper bound of the normal band, shown as the right scale tick.

props.name: absMin
props.description: Left end of the drawn axis. Defaults to 70% of the smaller of value and low when omitted, so the marker never sits on the edge.

props.name: absMax
props.description: Right end of the drawn axis. Defaults to 130% of the larger of value and high when omitted.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description: Accessible name for the img-role strip, e.g. the value against its reference range in words.

components.usage: <ReferenceRangeBar value={5.9} low={4} high={5.6} ariaLabel="HbA1c 5.9%, reference 4 to 5.6" />

anatomy.name: Track
anatomy.description: The full-width axis the marker travels along.

anatomy.name: Normal band
anatomy.description: The shaded span between low and high.

anatomy.name: Marker
anatomy.description: The value indicator; styled in-range or out-of-range.

anatomy.name: Scale
anatomy.description: The low and high numeric ticks under the track.

components.name: ScheduleStrip
components.summary: Compact row of day cells for medication adherence; each cell colours by its taken / missed / upcoming / paused status.

props.name: days
props.description: Ordered day cells. label is the short day name shown under each dot; status is one of taken, missed, upcoming, or paused and drives the dot colour.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description: Accessible name for the img-role list, e.g. a summary of the adherence week.

components.usage: <ScheduleStrip days={[{ label: "Mo", status: "taken" }, { label: "Tu", status: "missed" }]} ariaLabel="Adherence this week" />

bestPractices.text: Use taken and missed for past days, upcoming for future doses, and paused for a suspended course.
Keep labels to one or two characters (weekday initials) so the strip stays compact.
Drive this from a status value outside taken, missed, upcoming, or paused; unknown values get no styled dot.

components.name: Select
components.summary: Dropdown picker for one value from a list (Base UI select).

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Text shown on the trigger when nothing is chosen. Assumes string item values (value === label); for object values supply a custom Value.

props.name: children
props.description: The options as SelectItems.

props.name: className
props.description: Extra class(es) appended to the popup surface.

components.usage:
<Select defaultValue="7 days" placeholder="Duration">
  <SelectItem value="3 days">3 days</SelectItem>
  <SelectItem value="7 days">7 days</SelectItem>
</Select>

bestPractices.text: Use for one choice from a longer list (~6+); for a handful of always-visible options use a RadioGroup.
Use a Select to fire actions - a list of commands is a Menu, not a value picker.

anatomy.name: Trigger
anatomy.description: The closed control showing the current Value + a chevron.

anatomy.name: Item
anatomy.description: One option (SelectItem); a check marks the selected one.

components.name: SelectItem
components.summary: One option inside a Select.

props.name: children
props.description: The visible option label.

components.name: Separator
components.summary: A visual divider between sections or items (Base UI separator).

bestPractices.text: Use to group; often whitespace alone is enough - reach for a rule only when the grouping needs a hard edge.
Stack multiple separators for spacing - adjust margins/tokens instead.

anatomy.name: Rule
anatomy.description: The hairline; horizontal or vertical per orientation.

examples.name: Vertical
examples.description: Inside a flex row (e.g. between toolbar groups).

components.name: SidePanel
components.summary: Injectable assistant shell: five modes (floating/sidebar/fullscreen/bottomsheet/embedded) laying out Header / Body / Footer.

props.name: mode
props.description: How the panel is presented / anchored.

props.name: width
props.description: Sidebar-mode width in px (controlled); the resize handle updates it via onWidth.

props.name: onWidth
props.description: Fires while the sidebar resize handle is dragged, with the new width.

props.name: minWidth
props.description: Lower clamp for the sidebar resize.

props.name: maxWidth
props.description: Upper clamp for the sidebar resize (default min(900, 70vw)).

props.name: sheetHeight
props.description: Bottom-sheet height in px; the consumer measures its content and feeds it in.

props.name: sheetFull
props.description: Bottom-sheet has reached the fullscreen takeover threshold.

props.name: children
props.description: SidePanel.Header / SidePanel.Body / SidePanel.Footer.

anatomy.name: Body
anatomy.description: The scrollable content region with a fade-scrim (SidePanel.Body); forwards a ref + onScroll + an overlay slot.

anatomy.name: Footer
anatomy.description: Non-scrolling bottom rows (SidePanel.Footer), e.g. a context chip + composer.

anatomy.name: Resize
anatomy.description: The drag handle, auto-rendered in sidebar mode.

components.name: Slider
components.summary: Choose a number or range by dragging along a track (Base UI slider).

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Accessible name; forwarded to each range-input thumb (a range slider suffixes " minimum" / " maximum"). Required - a slider has no intrinsic label. Alternatively pass aria-labelledby (it propagates to the thumbs via the Root).

bestPractices.text: Always give the slider an accessible name (aria-label or aria-labelledby) - it has none by default.
Use when the approximate position matters more than the exact number; pair with a visible value readout when precision matters.
Use a Slider for a value that needs an exact typed entry (a dose, a price) - use a number field instead.

anatomy.name: Track
anatomy.description: The rail the thumb travels along.

anatomy.name: Indicator
anatomy.description: The filled portion from the start to the thumb.

examples.name: Range
examples.description: A [lo, hi] value renders two thumbs automatically.

components.name: Sparkline
components.summary: Inline SVG line chart with soft gradient fill; draws in currentColor so it inherits the card tone.

props.name: data
props.description: The ordered series to plot (oldest to newest). Renders nothing when empty; the axis auto-scales to the min and max.

props.name: width
props.description: Intrinsic viewBox width; the SVG itself scales to 100% of its container, so this only sets the drawing aspect ratio.

props.name: height
props.description: Rendered pixel height and viewBox height of the chart.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description: Accessible name for the img-role SVG, e.g. a spoken summary of the trend. Provide a value that does not just repeat the metric name.

components.name: SproutCard
components.summary: Stateful wrapper that owns the Seed / Sprout / Shoot stage state and applies the cross-stage view-transition animation. Render-prop based; per-object cards stay presentational.

props.name: initialStage
props.description: Starting fidelity in uncontrolled mode. Ignored once stage is passed (controlled mode).

props.name: stage
props.description: Controlled fidelity. When set, the host owns state and no internal view transition runs; pair with onStageChange.

props.name: onStageChange
props.description: Fired with the next stage before it applies. Required in controlled mode; also useful as a change listener while uncontrolled.

props.name: render
props.description: Render prop (stage, transitions) that returns the per-object card. transitions carries toSeed / toSprout / toShoot / toggle / expand / collapse / rooted.

props.name: onRooted
props.description: Invoked when the render prop calls transitions.rooted, signalling the native-application handoff (the Rooted stage lives outside this component).

props.name: className
props.description: Extra class names appended after the canonical shell + stage classes.

components.usage: <SproutCard initialStage="seed" render={(stage, t) => <VitalSignCard data={hr} stage={stage} onEdit={t.toShoot} />} />

bestPractices.text: Leave the component uncontrolled for the built-in view-transition choreography; drive it via the transitions helpers from the render prop.
Pass stage + onStageChange together when the host must own the stage; the internal transition is skipped in that mode.
Model the Rooted handoff as a fourth stage here; fire onRooted and navigate the host app instead.

components.name: SproutToneProvider
components.summary: Sets --card-tone (and optionally tint / tintStrong / on) on a wrapper so descendant cards inherit a tone via the CSS cascade. Cards that explicitly pass their own tone override the provider default.

props.name: tone
props.description: Named identity tone applied to the subtree via a krnl-tone--* class; descendant cards inherit it unless they set their own tone.

props.name: customColor
props.description: Colour escape hatch. A CSS colour string sets --card-tone; an object sets the matching --card-tone-* bindings (solid / tint / tintStrong / on) as inline vars.

props.name: className
props.description: Extra class names appended after the provider class.

components.name: Stack
components.summary: Vertical flow with a token gap (flex column).

props.name: gap
props.description: Space between children; resolves to var(--space-N).

props.name: align
props.description: Cross-axis alignment (align-items).

props.name: justify
props.description: Main-axis distribution (justify-content).

props.name: p
props.description: Padding step (var(--space-N)); px/py/pt.. also accepted.

props.name: bg
props.description: Surface token name -> var(--<bg>).

props.name: children
props.description: The stacked content.

components.name: StatusPill
components.summary: Interpretation pill (dot + label). Tone follows the status, not the card identity tone.

props.name: status
props.description: Clinical interpretation; drives the dot + label colour. Keep this orthogonal to the card tone, which encodes identity, not interpretation.

props.name: label
props.description: The pill text. Falls back to children when omitted.

props.name: children
props.description: Alternative to label; rendered as the pill text when label is not passed.

bestPractices.text: Drive the pill from the measurement interpretation (normal / critical / ...), independent of the card identity tone.
Pass the card identity tone into status; that conflates identity and interpretation.

components.name: Stepper
components.summary: Shoot-stage editable value (minus / value / plus). No save; the consumer provides the CTA in the actions slot.

props.name: unit
props.description: Small trailing unit shown after the editable value (e.g. "bpm").

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called with the new numeric value on each change; wraps the Base UI onValueChange and skips null (mid-edit) values.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description: Accessible name for the number field, since there is no visible label.

components.usage: <Stepper value={72} unit="bpm" min={30} max={220} onChange={setBpm} ariaLabel="Hartslag" />

components.name: Tab
components.summary: One selectable tab.

props.name: children
props.description: The tab label (one or two words).

components.name: TabList
components.summary: The row of Tabs with the active indicator.

props.name: children
props.description: The Tab elements.

components.name: TabPanel
components.summary: The content for one tab.

props.name: children
props.description: The panel content.

components.name: Tabs
components.summary: Switch between peer views in one space (Base UI tabs).

props.name: children
props.description: A TabList of Tabs followed by a TabPanel per value.

bestPractices.text: Use for a handful of peer views the user switches between; keep tab labels to one or two words.
Use Tabs for a linear multi-step flow - that is a stepper/wizard. Tabs imply the sections are independent and equal.

anatomy.name: List
anatomy.description: The row of tabs + the sliding indicator.

anatomy.name: Panel
anatomy.description: The content shown for the selected tab.

components.name: TextArea
components.summary: Multiline text field (native textarea on Kernel tokens).

props.name: rows
props.description: Initial visible height in text rows; the field still grows via native resize unless disabled in CSS.

props.name: bare
props.description: Strips the outlined-field paint so a bespoke class fully owns the look - the multiline sibling of Button variant="bare".

bestPractices.text: Size rows to the expected input; a comment box is ~3-4 rows, a long note more.
Use a TextArea for a single-line value (a name, a code) - that is a TextInput.

components.name: TextInput
components.summary: Outlined text field.

props.name: bare
props.description: Strips the outlined-field paint (border, radius, padding, fill, full width) so a bespoke class fully owns the look - the field sibling of Button variant="bare".

components.name: Tip
components.summary: Deprecated alias of Tooltip (B-39). Use Tooltip; Tip is removed on the next major.

components.name: Toggle
components.summary: On/off switch (Base UI).

components.name: ToggleGroup
components.summary: A set of related toggle buttons - a segmented control (Base UI toggle-group).

props.name: children
props.description: The ToggleGroupItem options.

bestPractices.text: Use for 2-4 mutually exclusive views/modes shown at once (a segmented control); keep labels short.
Use a ToggleGroup for one on/off setting - that is a Toggle (switch); or for many options - that is a Select.

anatomy.name: Item
anatomy.description: One toggle option (ToggleGroupItem); pressed state via [data-pressed].

components.name: ToggleGroupItem
components.summary: One option inside a ToggleGroup.

props.name: children
props.description: The option label.

components.name: Tooltip
components.summary: Tooltip wrapper (Base UI).

props.name: label
props.description: Short tooltip text shown on hover / focus of the trigger.

props.name: children
props.description: The trigger element the tooltip is attached to.

components.name: TrendChip
components.summary: Compact delta indicator with a direction arrow (up / down / flat).

props.name: direction
props.description: Movement direction; selects the arrow glyph and the chip colour treatment.

props.name: value
props.description: The delta magnitude shown beside the arrow (e.g. "+3" or "2 mmHg").

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible name for the chip; describes the trend for screen readers since the arrow is decorative.

anatomy.name: Arrow
anatomy.description: Decorative direction glyph set from direction.

components.name: ValueDisplay
components.summary: Big numeric value + small unit; the canonical value-slot content for vitals and labs.

props.name: value
props.description: The primary reading, rendered large (e.g. 74).

props.name: unit
props.description: Small trailing unit shown after the value (e.g. "bpm").

props.name: prefix
props.description: Optional small marker before the value (e.g. a comparator like "<").

components.name: VitalSignCard
components.summary: FHIR Observation (vital-signs) at Seed/Sprout/Shoot. Handles 9 properties incl. blood pressure (multi-component).

props.name: data
props.description: The FHIR Observation projection: { property, value, unit, status, history, measuredAt, reference, trend }. For bloodPressure, value is { systolic, diastolic }; for others value is a number.

props.name: stage
props.description: Fidelity forwarded to the underlying Card. Sprout adds sparkline plus trend; Shoot swaps the value for an editable Stepper (except bloodPressure).

props.name: onSave
props.description: Invoked with the new value when the Shoot-stage Stepper changes or the save CTA fires.

props.name: onEdit
props.description: Invoked when the user taps the edit chip in Sprout stage.
